page: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 server instance belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core, and includes 1920 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scoring 2168.0 and gzip compression reaching 31.37 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7995.24 and a Passmark CPU score of 14267.95. Memory bandwidth is measured at 621.16 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 65.68 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For LLM workloads, the instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 270.03 tokens/sec on small 135M models, but performance degrades to a weak 8.36 tokens/sec on medium 7B models, highlighting a tradeoff in handling larger model parameters.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L8s_v4 offers a high local storage-to-vCPU ratio and dense memory allocation, making it a viable option for workloads requiring high-capacity local storage without the overhead of larger compute instances. The hardware configuration is well-suited for database operations, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 5261.65 and Redis SET performance of 11.48 million operations per second. It is also appropriate for text processing, software compilation, and general-purpose storage-heavy workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1920 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2168.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 31.37 GB/sec. However, multi-core and large-block memory write benchmarks remain average, and LLM text generation performance drops significantly on medium-sized models. With its high local storage capacity and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, the L8s_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-intensive tasks. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, text processing, and software compilation workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB of local storage, optimized for database operations and storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB local storage, designed for database and storage workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, dense memory, and high-capacity local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory and 1920 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage
Strong single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2168.0
Average multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth write speeds
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and text processing
